headline.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
headline.summary: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

creative.title: Microsoft launches $2.5 billion "Frontier Company" to embed 6,000 AI engineers inside enterprise clients
creative.summary: Microsoft is investing $2.5 billion in a new unit called "Frontier Company" that puts 6,000 engineers directly at enterprise customers. The goal is to integrate AI into core processes with measurable ROI, not more experimentation. Microsoft is positi
creative.content:
Microsoft is investing $2.5 billion in a new unit called "Frontier Company" that puts 6,000 engineers directly at enterprise customers. The goal is to integrate AI into core processes with measurable ROI, not more experimentation. Microsoft is positioning itself as a platform-neutral alternative to OpenAI and Anthropic, which push their own models through their own deployment companies.

creative.title: Anthropic reportedly explores custom chip manufacturing with Samsung while insisting Nvidia still matters
creative.summary: Anthropic is reportedly in talks with Samsung Electronics about manufacturing a custom AI chip. The project is still early, but Anthropic has already hired chip engineers. After OpenAI's "Jalapeño," yet another major AI company is pushing into chip d
creative.content:
Anthropic is reportedly in talks with Samsung Electronics about manufacturing a custom AI chip. The project is still early, but Anthropic has already hired chip engineers. After OpenAI's "Jalapeño," yet another major AI company is pushing into chip development to cut infrastructure costs.
The article Anthropic reportedly explores custom chip manufacturing with Samsung while insisting Nvidia still matters appeared first on The Decoder.

creative.title: Anthropic says it cut 80 percent of Claude Code's system prompt because Fable 5 models "want a smaller system prompt"
creative.summary: Anthropic has cut the system prompt for Claude Code by 80 percent. According to staffer Tariq Shihipar, the new Fable 5 models need fewer instructions and examples. Guidelines can even hold the models back because they're "more imaginative" than what
creative.content:
Anthropic has cut the system prompt for Claude Code by 80 percent. According to staffer Tariq Shihipar, the new Fable 5 models need fewer instructions and examples. Guidelines can even hold the models back because they're "more imaginative" than what they're given. Instead of strict rules, Anthropic now steers through context.
The article Anthropic says it cut 80 percent of Claude Code's system prompt because Fable 5 models "want a smaller system prompt" appeared first on The Decoder.

research.content: Frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management (BPM) first gained traction because they promised clarity in the chaos—a structured way to bring order to messy, sprawling operations. Lean Six Sigma emphasized statistical rigor and quality control; BPM created end-to-end maps of how work should flow across departments. Both offered a repeatable way to…

research.content: Artificial intelligence may have captured the public imagination through chatbots and image generators, but some of its most consequential use cases are unfolding far from consumer-facing tools. In industries where physical infrastructure, operational continuity, and safety are paramount, AI is becoming a core operating layer. research.title: Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
research.summary: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas, encouraging the dairy sector to produce a gas we burn
research.content: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas, encouraging the dairy sector to produce a gas we burn instead of one that just pollutes the air. It’s become wildly popular because…

research.title: Constructive Alignment: Governing Preference Dynamics in Human-AI Interaction
research.summary:
arXiv:2607.00001v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Most approaches to AI alignment treat human preferences as fixed targets to be inferred and optimized. This assumption conflicts with extensive empirical evidence showing that preferences are layered,
research.content:
arXiv:2607.00001v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Most approaches to AI alignment treat human preferences as fixed targets to be inferred and optimized. This assumption conflicts with extensive empirical evidence showing that preferences are layered, dynamic, and constructed through interaction--particularly with adaptive technologies. As AI systems become more persistent, personalized, and socially embedded, they increasingly participate in shaping what people attend to, value, and endorse over
